best.explanation: Belgas looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 177 kJ, 12 s period, workable consistency 53/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a clean fun-sized window for Fun progression, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 115 kJ, 12 s period, workable consistency 54/100, and good wind at 5 km/h.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small.  Expect: it can get busy, and it is usually more flow than heavy barrel.

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 94 kJ, 13 s period, workable consistency 52/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean small window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 63 kJ, 12 s period, lully 39/100, and good wind at 15 km/h.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort.  Watch: spot surf looks too small.
